root_text.an10.77:1.3: Dhaṁsī ca, pagabbho ca, tintiṇo ca, mahagghaso ca, luddo ca, akāruṇiko ca, dubbalo ca, oravitā ca, muṭṭhassati ca, necayiko ca—
root_text.an10.77:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato kāko.
root_text.an10.77:1.5: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhu.
root_text.an10.77:1.7: Dhaṁsī ca, pagabbho ca, tintiṇo ca, mahagghaso ca, luddo ca, akāruṇiko ca, dubbalo ca, oravitā ca, muṭṭhassati ca, necayiko ca—
root_text.an10.77:1.8: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhū”ti.

translation_text.an10.77:1.1: “Mendicants, a crow has ten bad qualities.
translation_text.an10.77:1.3: They’re rude and impudent, gluttonous and voracious, cruel and pitiless, weak and raucous, unmindful and acquisitive.
translation_text.an10.77:1.4: A crow possesses these ten bad qualities.
translation_text.an10.77:1.5: In the same way, a bad mendicant possesses these ten bad qualities.
translation_text.an10.77:1.7: They’re rude and impudent, gluttonous and voracious, cruel and pitiless, weak and raucous, unmindful and acquisitive.
translation_text.an10.77:1.8: A bad mendicant possesses these ten bad qualities.”

variant_text.an10.77:1.3: tintiṇo → tintino (bj); nillajjo (mr); tintiṇoti tintiṇaṁ vuccati taṇhā … (sya-a) | necayiko → nerasiko (si)
